title: Sprint demo flow contract
summary: A reference example that shows how sprint demos should capture entry points, the intended user journey, reviewable screens, and design-system usage before implementation starts.

entryPoints.label: Calendar surface
entryPoints.location: Month view or settings surface where the feature naturally appears.
entryPoints.userIntent: The user notices a contextual action without leaving the primary calendar task.

entryPoints.label: Prompt or control
entryPoints.location: A visible button, sheet, banner, or menu item that starts the feature.
entryPoints.userIntent: The user understands why this action is available and what will happen next.

flowSteps.label: Discover
flowSteps.userAction: User reaches the feature from the normal product path.
flowSteps.systemResponse: The app presents a calm, contextual call to action.

flowSteps.label: Start
flowSteps.userAction: User chooses the primary action or backs out.
flowSteps.systemResponse: The app opens the smallest screen needed to continue the task.

flowSteps.label: Complete
flowSteps.systemResponse: The app returns to the right product location with the new state visible.

screens.name: Entry screen
screens.primaryAction: Start the feature from the calendar context.
screens.secondaryAction: Ignore the prompt and keep using the current screen.
stateNotes: The entry point should not hide the user's existing calendar task.
The call to action should use shared button, surface, and spacing tokens.

screens.name: Decision screen
screens.secondaryAction: Cancel and return to the entry point.
stateNotes: The user should know what will change before confirming.
The cancel path should preserve the user's previous context.

screens.name: Result screen
screens.primaryAction: Continue from the updated product state.
screens.secondaryAction: Review or adjust the result if the feature needs it.
stateNotes: The completed state should be visible without a toast-only confirmation.
Any follow-up action should feel optional, not required.

components: Existing calendar spacing and foreground tokens
notes: Demo pages should avoid production data, auth, persistence, and API calls.
Custom UI is allowed only when the gap is recorded for the design system.

reviewChecklist: The feature entry point is obvious but not disruptive.
The user can move from start to completion without a missing screen.
Cancel, empty, or fallback states are represented when they affect the flow.
The demo names the design-system components and tokens it relies on.
Any visual inconsistency is captured before implementation planning.
